preview.summary: Belgium's golden-generation core opens Group G against a confident Egypt side built around Mohamed Salah. Expect Belgium to dominate possession while Egypt look to strike on the counter through their Liverpool talisman.
preview.homeForm: Belgium qualified unbeaten from UEFA, topping their group with 18 points and 29 goals, and arrive ranked 9th in the world.
preview.awayForm: Egypt cruised through African qualifying with a near-flawless campaign, topping their group with a game to spare.
preview.homeKey: Kevin De Bruyne, creative engine playing in his fourth World Cup.
preview.awayKey: Mohamed Salah, qualifying's joint top African scorer with nine goals.
storylines: De Bruyne and Salah, ex-Premier League rivals, square off
Belgium chasing redemption after recent tournament flops
Egypt eyeing a first-ever World Cup knockout berth
preview.h2h: Belgium and Egypt have met three times since 2005, with Egypt winning two, though Belgium took the last meeting 3-0; they have never faced off at a World Cup.
